name: Chewy Fig Oatmeal Cookies
recipeInstructions:
[NOTE: For a no-sugar variation, substitute the same amount of white grape juice concentrate or apple juice concentrate for brown sugar.]
1. In a large bowl, beat together brown sugar and honey until creamy.
2. Add margarine and mix with an electric mixer for 3-5 minutes.
3. Incorporate egg substitute (or eggs) into the creamed mixture.
4. Mix baking soda, salt, cinnamon, vanilla, remaining figs, and nutmeg in a separate bowl.
5. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture.
6. Stir in flour, oatmeal, and nuts (if using).
7. Drop batter by spoonfuls onto a greased cookie sheet.
8. Bake at 350°F for 10-15 minutes or until cookies are soft or crunchy to your liking.
Each serving contains:
Calories: 74
Fat: 2.01 g
Dietary Fiber: 0.936 g
Carbohydrates: 13.0 g
Protein: 1.45 g
Sodium: 100 mg
Cholesterol: 0.031 mg
